titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/235

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=570 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:07:54.76559+00 | translation moa=571 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:08:01.794465+00

p.span:
[?Vniuersis Christi fidelibus presentes audituris litter]as uel [uisuris] Patricius Comes de Dunbar salutem in domino . Sciant presentes et futuri nos concessisse et hac carta nostra confirmasse donacionem illam quam Magister
Willelmus fecit in curia nostra apud Edirham Abbati et Conuentui de Melros tanquam suis assignatis . de quadam terra sita in territorio de Halsingtune . de qua Idem Magister
Willelmus ... se dissaisiuit . et ipsos sollempniter insaisiuit . quam quidem terram Robertus de Muscampo tenens noster dedit dicto Magistro Willelmo . sibi et suis assignatis iure perpetuo possidendam . Te
nend’ et habend’ . Dictis abbati et conuentui in liberam . puram et perpetuam elemosinam per illas diuisas per quas dictus Magister Willelmus eam tenuit . et cum omnibus pertinenciis . libertatibus et aisiamentis ad eam perti
nentibus et cum communi pastura dicte ville de Halsingtune et cum libero intuitu et exitu ad eandem pasturam et ad dicta aisiamenta  adeo integre . libere . quiete . et plenarie ab omni seculari seruicio . exaccione .
consuetudine et demanda . sicut aliqua elemosina in regno Scocie liberius . quiecius . plenius et honorificencius tenetur aut possidetur . Et ut Idem abbas et Conuentus super eadem terra non ualeant futuris temporibus
ab aliquo molestari  nos et heredes nostri pro salute animarum nostrarum eandem terram per dictas diuisas suas cum omnibus premissis . Ipsis abbati et conuentui contra omnes homines inperpetuum warantizabimus .
acquietabimus et defendemus Et ad hoc [?faciliter] et sine difficultate qualibet faciendum  nos et heredes nostros predictis abbati et conuentui per has nostras patentes litteras obligamus . Actum apud Berewic .
in presencia karissimi ... ... ... octodecimo die aprilis anno regni eiusdem tricesimo quarto . Test’ . Roberto de Ros’. Dauid de Graham . Roberto de Meiners . Willelmo filio Co
mitis . Thom’ . de Normanuill’ ... Henrico de Graham . Nicholao Corbeth . Waltero filio Walteri Senescalli . Roberto filio Comitis . Thom’ . de Boscho . et Bernardo de Ryppelei . et
...

div.p: [To the entirety of Christ's faithful people, who shall hear or view the present letters,] Patrick, earl of Dunbar sends greeting in the Lord. Let present and future know us to have granted and by this our charter to have made firm the donation that Master William made in our court at Edrom to the abbot and monastic community of Melrose as well as to their assigns, of a land to be found in the territory of Hassington, of which Master William ... dispossessed himself and solemnly gave them saisine, and it was this land that Robert de Muschamp, our tenant, gave to the said Master William for him and his assigns to possess by everlasting right; for the said abbot and monastic community to have and to hold in free, pure and everlasting alms by the boundaries by which the said Master William held it, and with all pertinents, liberties and easements pertaining to it, and with the common pasture of the said toun of Hassington and with free entrance and exit to the the same pasture and to the said easements, as wholly, freely, quitely, and fully from every secular service, exaction, custom and demand as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is freely, quitely, fully and honourably held and possessed. And so that the abbot and monastic community should not have to be troubled in times to come by any one in respect of the same land, we and our heirs for the salvation of our souls shall guarantee, release from obligation, and protect the same land by its said boundaries, with everything aforementioned, for the abbot and monastic community against all men for ever. And in order to do this [easily] and without any difficulty, we and our heirs bind ourselves to the aforesaid abbot and monastic community by these letters patent. Done at Berwick in the presence of the well-beloved ... ... ... on the eighteenth day of April in the thirty-fourth year of the same king. As witness: Robert of Roos; David Graham; Robert Menzies; William the earl's son; Thomas de Normanville ... ... ... Henry Graham; Nicholas Corbett; Walter son of Walter the Steward; Robert the earl's son; Thomas del Bois, Bernard of Ripley, and ... ... ...
